data.description: env vars should be stored in .env, with examples in .env.example
data.title: set up dot env for local testing.

data.body:
Reviewed commit 949a6df (.env.example, .gitignore, server/tools/run-dev.sh, README.md).

Acceptance criteria:
- .env.example exists at repo root, tracked, lists all 5 vars (PORT, DATA_DIR, PRINTFUL_TOKEN, PRINTFUL_STORE_ID, PUBLIC_BASE_URL) with comments and matching defaults: MET.
- .env is gitignored. git check-ignore -v .env returns positive match: MET.
- .gitignore ignores .env / .env.* with !.env.example allowlist; verified .env and .env.local match, .env.example does NOT match: MET.
- server/tools/run-dev.sh exists, chmod +x (-rwxr-xr-x), sources <repo>/.env when present, skips when absent. Booted via wrapper, hit /healthz -> ok. Booted with .env temporarily renamed -> still loads with defaults: MET.
- README 'Run the server locally' step 3 documents cp .env.example .env -> edit -> ./tools/run-dev.sh, with manual set -a; source form as alternative, env-var list preserved: MET.
- Env-var inventory cross-check: grep os.Getenv/LookupEnv across server/ finds exactly the 5 vars in main.go; .env.example covers all 5 with no extras: MET.
- go test ./... from server/ -> all packages pass: MET.
- Commit subject 'TYB-10: feat(dev): scaffold local .env workflow' follows TYB-11 prefix convention: MET.
- Doc gate: README updated for the new run command: MET.

No issues found.

Verdict: Pass — no issues found.
